changes: DFA a1 threshold detection rebuilt (v11.44–v11.46 / v3.113–v3.115) — LT1/LT2 estimates now report only from a sustained contiguous effort (no more phantom thresholds from scattered data); aerobic-threshold marker corrected to the literature value (HRVT1 at DFA a1 0.75, per Rogers et al.); three self-describing markers — easy-state guard, LT1, LT2 — each with a reason code when withheld; full 14-day calibration window restored; time-in-zone bands renamed to match.

Readiness alert engine hardened (v11.48–v11.49 / v3.116–v3.117) — 'Tier 1' terminology disambiguated (it had drifted across four meanings) and the full alerts[] schema documented; P0/P1 overload/safety skips now reference only the signals that actually triggered them; P1 persistent-skip gated on alert severity. Reliability/correctness — no change to recommendations on current data.
Display-unit blocks (v11.40 / v3.109) — report narrative renders metrics in your preferred units.
Season Report tier + prerequisites (v11.41–v11.42 / sync.py v3.110) — new season-level report, with weekly capability rollups and monthly phase aggregation feeding it.
Block W/kg headline + weekly weight-trend (v11.43 / v3.112) — block reports lead with power-to-weight; weekly weight trend surfaced as a signal.
Freshness fix (v3.111) — latest.json history.last_generated no longer goes stale after an auto-history rebuild.

Upgrade: after replacing sync.py, run sync twice manually, or wait for two scheduled runs to complete. If history does not regenerate, force it with --generate-history or delete history.json and rerun.
